channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Nathan Lane says 'Death of a Salesman' is 'the most rewarding thing I've done'
item.description: Lane is nominated for a Tony for his portrayal of Arthur Miller's iconic salesman: "When people come back and talk about it, if they're not weeping, they're saying things like, 'You were my father.'"
item.content:encoded:
'/>

Lane is nominated for a Tony for his portrayal of Arthur Miller's iconic salesman: "When people come back and talk about it, if they're not weeping, they're saying things like, 'You were my father.'"

(Image credit: Emilio Madrid)
item.dc:creator: Sam Fragoso

item.title: Dirty nickel: The cost of mining in Indonesia
item.description: Across six locations in Indonesia, NPR spoke with locals about how nickel mining is changing the land and daily life. It's brought jobs, but also concerns about environmental damage and public health.
item.content:encoded:
Across six locations in Indonesia, NPR spoke with locals about how nickel mining is changing the land and daily life. It's brought jobs, but also concerns about environmental damage and public health.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Katerina Barton

item.title: The real-world influence of Star Trek's U.S.S. Enterprise
item.description: The Star Trek Enterprise evoked Americans' sense of adventure and a utopian vision of humanity. It's part of NPR's 250th anniversary series "America in Pursuit."
item.content:encoded: The Star Trek Enterprise evoked Americans' sense of adventure and a utopian vision of humanity. It's part of NPR's 250th anniversary series "America in Pursuit."
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Watch boys go from frightened to feral in an unforgettable 'Lord of the Flies'
item.description: A TV adaptation of William Golding's 1954 novel follows schoolboys who survive a plane crash and must fend for themselves on a deserted island. It was co-written by Adolescence creator Jack Thorne.
item.content:encoded:
A TV adaptation of William Golding's 1954 novel follows schoolboys who survive a plane crash and must fend for themselves on a deserted island. It was co-written by Adolescence creator Jack Thorne.

(Image credit: J Redza/Eleven/Sony Pictures Television)
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: 'A trailblazer, a rabble-rouser, a do-gooder': CNN founder Ted Turner dies at 87
item.description: Turner had a larger-than-life personality, and used it to launch the country's first 24/7 all-news network.
item.content:encoded:
Turner had a larger-than-life personality, and used it to launch the country's first 24/7 all-news network.

(Image credit: Rick Diamond/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: New book imagines Hans Christian Andersen showing up to Charles Dickens' house
item.description: In Five Weeks in the Country, author Francine Prose imagines a distressing, ill-timed visit to Charles Dickens' home in 1857 — offering a memorable twist on the classic English country house drama.
item.content:encoded: In Five Weeks in the Country, author Francine Prose imagines a distressing, ill-timed visit to Charles Dickens' home in 1857 — offering a memorable twist on the classic English country house drama.
item.dc:creator: Heller McAlpin

item.title: Do you say 'wash' or 'warsh?' Here's where the pronunciation comes from
item.description: Trump's nominee for the Federal Reserve chair, Kevin Warsh, has dominated headlines for weeks. For some, his surname is a reminder of a disappearing accent in South Midland America.
item.content:encoded:
Trump's nominee for the Federal Reserve chair, Kevin Warsh, has dominated headlines for weeks. For some, his surname is a reminder of a disappearing accent in South Midland America.

(Image credit: Fox Photos/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Napheesa Collier says her critique of the WNBA was 'a little dramatic,' but it worked
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks WNBA star Napheesa Collier about the league's new contract with the players and the state of women's sports.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks WNBA star Napheesa Collier about the league's new contract with the players and the state of women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Scott Turow's latest real-life legal thriller: Suing Meta for copyright infringement
item.description: Five major publishing houses and the bestselling author are suing Meta and its CEO Mark Zuckerberg for allegedly training its Llama generative AI models on millions of copyrighted materials.
item.content:encoded:
Five major publishing houses and the bestselling author are suing Meta and its CEO Mark Zuckerberg for allegedly training its Llama generative AI models on millions of copyrighted materials.

(Image credit: Thos Robinson)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: A legal scholar and 'Backtalker' defends critical race theory — a term she helped coin
item.description: Kimberlé Williams Crenshaw helped name two contested ideas in U.S. politics — intersectionality and critical race theory. Her memoir chronicles the personal and legal framework for her thinking.
item.content:encoded:
Kimberlé Williams Crenshaw helped name two contested ideas in U.S. politics — intersectionality and critical race theory. Her memoir chronicles the personal and legal framework for her thinking.

(Image credit: Carl Timpone)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley
